entries.name: /worktree
entries.description: that creates a separate git worktree so changes happen in isolation.

entries.name: /best-of-n
entries.description: that runs the same task in parallel across multiple models, each in its own isolated worktree, then compares outcomes.

entries.name: /plan
entries.description: takes you back to your current plan and its action menu. We've also added keyboard shortcuts in the prompt bar so you can use arrow keys to navigate options,

entries.name: /resume
entries.description: now sorts by last interaction time, not creation time, so your most recent conversations appear first.

entries.description: , and similar commands now toggle with a single invocation. Current status is shown in the command description.

entries.name: claude-in-chrome
entries.description: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension).

entries.name: debug
entries.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

entries.name: keybindings-help
entries.description: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

entries.name: simplify
entries.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.

entries.name: update-config
entries.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.

entries.name: dream
entries.description: Reflective memory consolidation — review recent activity, synthesize learnings into typed memory files, and prune stale entries.

entries.name: schedule
entries.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule.

entries.name: claude-api
entries.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
